$comment: The card and the front-desk page read the HTML template, the brand fonts and
two npm dist files off disk at request time. Vercel's bundler traces static
imports; it cannot see a path built at runtime, so those files would be left
out of the deployment and the card would 500 in production while rendering
perfectly on a laptop. includeFiles is what stops that.
$crons_comment: 06:00 UTC is 08:00 in Johannesburg — the owner's arrivals list lands before
the front desk opens, and tomorrow's guests get their reminder at a civilised
hour. Vercel's free tier allows one daily cron, which is all this needs:
expiring an abandoned hold is deliberately NOT on this schedule, because
check_availability does it on every call. The system stays correct even if
this cron never runs at all.
